note: casa = house or home. "a casa" (to home) you already saw in My Day: voy a casa.
Vivo en un apartamento = I live in an apartment. In Spain you also hear "piso".
A room in general, and often "bedroom" too: mi habitación = my room.
One word for both: hay una cocina = there is a kitchen; hay dos baños = there are two bathrooms. It never changes.
Use está for where one thing is, están for several: el baño está arriba; los dormitorios están abajo.
Hay una cocina grande = there is a big kitchen. cocina also means "I cook" (from cocinar).
From dormir (to sleep) - the room you sleep in.
El baño está arriba = the bathroom is upstairs.
The main shared room. Hay un salón grande = there is a big living room.
A floor/storey of a building: la primera planta = the first floor.
Where something is: el baño está arriba = the bathroom is upstairs.
The opposite of arriba: la cocina está abajo = the kitchen is downstairs.
Vivo solo = I live alone. A woman says "sola".
Vivo con mi familia = I live with my family.
mis (plural "my") because amigos is plural: con mis amigos = with my friends.
